Accessibility Findings

https://fire.airnow.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 84

Axe findings: 3

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-3b502305

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~561People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~561 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 56,094 affected page loads) ~168People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~168 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 56,094 affected page loads)

Affected elements (4):

Element 1 DAP-92ee780a

Element path: nav.navbar > div.container-fluid > div.d-flex > button.btn

Snippet:

<button class="btn btn-primary nav-btn svelte-afbvbm" type="button" data-bs-toggle="offcanvas" data-bs-target="#offcanvasInfo" aria-controls="offcanvasInfo">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Element 1 DAP-eede14fd

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,height=device-heightinitial-scale=1,maximum-scale=1,use…">

Accessibility Findings

https://bwt.cbp.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 89

Axe findings: 4

ARIA input fields do not have accessible names (rule: aria-input-field-name) DAP-07fb7abc

When an input field doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn more about input field labels.

Disabilities affected: ~554People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~554 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 55,363 affected page loads) ~166People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,273 people potentially excluded (2.3% prevalence × 55,363 affected page loads)

  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty

  • Element has no title attribute
Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-8873dcca

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~1,273 people potentially excluded (2.3% prevalence × 55,363 affected page loads) ~2KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~2,381 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 55,363 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-2dc5fa09

Element path: div.MuiFormControl-root > div.MuiInputBase-root > div.MuiSelect-select > span.text-white

Snippet:

<span class="text-white opacity-90">

Label: Canadian Border Ports of Entry

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 4.47 (foreground color: #e6f1f8, background color: #0071bc, font size: 10.5pt (14px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Element 2 DAP-2dc5fa09

Element path: div.MuiFormControl-root > div.MuiInputBase-root > div.MuiSelect-select > span.text-white

Snippet:

<span class="text-white opacity-90">

Label: Mexican Border Ports of Entry

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 4.47 (foreground color: #e6f1f8, background color: #0071bc, font size: 10.5pt (14px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-7471b5ac

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~1,273 people potentially excluded (2.3% prevalence × 55,363 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~3,211 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 55,363 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-a6d37d77

Element path: div.min-h-screen > footer.w-full > div.mx-auto > a.hover:underline

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.cbp.gov/site-policy-notices/foia" class="hover:underline whitespace-nowrap" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">

Label: FOIA

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (28px by 16px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 18.6px instead of at least 24px.

Element 2 DAP-a6d37d77

Element path: div.min-h-screen > footer.w-full > div.mx-auto > a.hover:underline

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.cbp.gov/site-policy-notices" class="hover:underline whitespace-nowrap" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">

Label: Site Policies

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (65.4px by 16px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 18.6px instead of at least 24px.
Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch) DAP-cce963f5

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.
